name: lmeb
description: Benchmark embedding models on long-horizon memory retrieval across episodic, dialogue, semantic, and procedural memory types. Identify which embeddings excel at agent memory tasks vs. traditional passage retrieval. Guide selection of embedding models for memory-heavy agent systems.
keywords: embedding models, memory retrieval, long-horizon reasoning, benchmark, agent memory
content:
# LMEB: Long-Horizon Memory Embedding Benchmark

Standard embedding benchmarks (MTEB) evaluate passage retrieval—short, static queries. Agent memory tasks differ: retrieve relevant memories from long interaction histories, dialogue, procedures. LMEB evaluates embeddings on four memory types across 22 datasets, revealing that larger models don't always perform better and MTEB doesn't predict agent-memory performance.

## Why This Matters for Agents

Agents increasingly maintain memory: conversation history, task procedures, facts learned. Choosing the right embedding model for memory retrieval is critical but unmeasured. LMEB provides a benchmark to evaluate embeddings specifically for agent memory tasks, not generic passage retrieval.

## Core Concept

Memory types: (1) Episodic (what happened), (2) Dialogue (conversation context), (3) Semantic (facts/knowledge), (4) Procedural (how to do things). Different embeddings excel at different types. LMEB quantifies these differences.

## Implementation

```python
class AgentMemoryRetriever:
    def __init__(self, embedding_model='sentence-transformers/all-mpnet-base-v2'):
        self.embedder = SentenceTransformer(embedding_model)
        self.memory_store = {}
        self.memory_types = {}
    
    def store_memory(self, memory_text, memory_type='episodic'):
        """Store memory with type."""
        embedding = self.embedder.encode(memory_text)
        memory_id = len(self.memory_store)
        self.memory_store[memory_id] = embedding
        self.memory_types[memory_id] = memory_type
        return memory_id
    
    def retrieve(self, query, top_k=5, memory_type=None):
        """Retrieve relevant memories."""
        query_embedding = self.embedder.encode(query)
        
        # Compute similarities
        similarities = {}
        for mem_id, mem_embedding in self.memory_store.items():
            if memory_type and self.memory_types[mem_id] != memory_type:
                continue
            sim = cosine_similarity(query_embedding, mem_embedding)
            similarities[mem_id] = sim
        
        # Return top-k
        top_memories = sorted(similarities.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)[:top_k]
        return top_memories
```

## When to Use

- Selecting embeddings for agent memory systems
- Evaluating memory retrieval performance
- Multi-turn dialogue agents

## Reference

- **Paper**: arXiv:2603.12572 (March 2026). LMEB by Xinping Zhao et al.
- **Benchmark**: 22 datasets, 193 zero-shot tasks across 4 memory types
- **Finding**: Larger models ≠ better on long-horizon; LMEB orthogonal to MTEB
